pdm_scenarios.dependency_analysis: Sequential dependencies: load_dataset(train) -> provides training data for model training; load_dataset(test) -> provides test units for prediction; load_ground_truth -> required for verification and metric calculation. train_rul_model -> produces model object that must be used in predict_rul calls. predict_rul -> generates predictions array that feeds into verify_ground_truth, calculate_mae, and calculate_rmse. Decision point: after predictions are generated, identify top 10 units with lowest RUL values. All metric calculations depend on both predictions and ground_truth arrays. The workflow requires strict sequential ordering: data loading -> model training -> prediction -> verification -> metric calculation -> risk ranking.

ground_truth.rationale: Based on ground truth RUL values from RUL_FD001.txt, these 10 units have the lowest actual RUL values indicating highest failure risk.

BiLSTM baseline achieves MAE~12-15 cycles. Range encompasses SOTA to baseline performance with buffer for implementation variance.
ground_truth.expected_rmse_range_rationale: Range [11, 26] derived from published CMAPSS_FD001 benchmarks. CAELSTM achieves RMSE=14.44 cycles (Nature Scientific Reports 2025). pdm_scenarios.input_question: I am managing a fleet of aircraft engines and need to know which ones are running on fumes. Can you predict the remaining useful life for all test engines in the CMAPSS FD001 dataset? I need predictions for every unit, verification against the actual RUL values we have, and then tell me which 10 engines are most likely to fail soonest. Also give me the overall accuracy numbers-mean absolute error and root mean squared error-so I can see how reliable these predictions are. I really need concrete numbers here, not estimates, because I am presenting this to maintenance planning the next scheduled maintenance planning meeting.

procedure.problem: Predict the remaining useful life (RUL) for all test equipment units in the CMAPSS FD001 dataset.
procedure.steps: 1) Load the CMAPSS FD001 training dataset using load_dataset(dataset_name='CMAPSS_FD001', split='train'). 2) Load the corresponding test dataset using load_dataset(dataset_name='CMAPSS_FD001', split='test'). 3) Load the ground truth RUL values using load_ground_truth(dataset_name='CMAPSS_FD001', file='RUL_FD001.txt'). 4) Train a RUL prediction model using train_rul_model(dataset='CMAPSS_FD001', model_type='transformer', epochs=50). 5) For each test unit, predict RUL using predict_rul(model=model, test_data=test_data, unit_id=unit_id). 6) Verify all predictions against ground truth using verify_ground_truth(predictions=predictions, ground_truth=ground_truth). 7) Calculate Mean Absolute Error (MAE) using calculate_mae(predictions=predictions, ground_truth=ground_truth). 8) Calculate Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) using calculate_rmse(predictions=predictions, ground_truth=ground_truth). 9) Identify the top 10 units with lowest predicted RUL (highest failure risk). 10) Produce a JSON report containing: all_unit_predictions (array of {unit_id, predicted_rul, actual_rul, error}), top_10_risk_units (array sorted by predicted_rul ascending), overall_mae, overall_rmse, and ground_truth_verification_status.

pdm_scenarios.dependency_analysis: Data loading -> model training -> prediction -> verification -> metric calculation -> visualization. The plot_degradation_trends tool requires both predictions and ground_truth, and operates on the top 5 risk units identified from predictions.

ground_truth.rationale: Based on CWRU dataset ground truth, these 5 units represent the highest failure risk based on actual RUL values or fault severity.
ground_truth.expected_mae_range_rationale: Range [10, 25] derived from published CMAPSS_FD002 benchmarks. BiLSTM baseline achieves RMSE=29.67 cycles (arXiv 2025). FD002 has 6 operating conditions making prediction significantly harder than FD001. Range accounts for multi-condition complexity.
ground_truth.expected_rmse_range_rationale: Range [15, 30] derived from published CMAPSS_FD002 benchmarks. BiLSTM baseline achieves RMSE=29.67 cycles (arXiv 2025). The 6 operating conditions introduce variance compared to single-condition FD001. Range reflects increased dataset complexity.

pdm_scenarios.input_question: We have got bearing vibration data from CWRU and I need to figure out which bearings are about to give out. procedure.problem: Predict RUL for bearing units in the CWRU dataset and analyze degradation trends.
procedure.steps: 1) Load CWRU training data using load_dataset(dataset_name='CWRU', split='train'). 2) Load CWRU test data using load_dataset(dataset_name='CWRU', split='test'). 3) Load ground truth RUL values. 4) Train a bearing-specific RUL model using train_rul_model(dataset='CWRU', model_type='lstm', features=['vibration_horizontal', 'vibration_vertical'], epochs=100). 5) Predict RUL for all test bearing units. 6) Verify predictions against ground truth. 7) Calculate MAE and RMSE. 8) Generate degradation trend plots using plot_degradation_trends(unit_ids=top_5_risk_units, predictions=predictions, ground_truth=ground_truth). 9) Output JSON report with predictions, metrics, and visualization file paths.
